title: Baker's Dozen-and-Then-Some: Alex Goes Record-Hunting in the Algarve
title_candidates: Baker's Dozen-and-Then-Some: Alex Goes Record-Hunting in the Algarve
The Algarve Ascent: How Alex Baker Took TEG 10 to a New High
Forty-Nine, Fifty-One: Mullin Sets the Bar, Baker Vaults It
theme: Alex Baker chases down David Mullin's early onslaught with three successive personal bests, climaxing in a 51-point Boavista demolition that sets a new TEG single-round record — while Mullin, unbothered, strolls off with the Green Jacket and Patterson collects the Spoon he never looked like dropping.
opening_hook: Open on Boavista, Sunday, the 5th tee: Alex Baker about to embark on the six-hole stretch (b04) that will rewrite the TEG record book — then rewind to Saturday morning, when David Mullin still had this thing under control.
foreshadow: Mullin's opening 49 (b03) is flagged as the new benchmark — only for Baker to topple it three days later (b02).
Alex Baker's quiet runner-up position after R1, eight back, planted as the seed of the comeback.
Patterson's R1 H4 slide to the bottom of the Spoon table foreshadowed as a position he will never relinquish.
Two Stuart Neumann sextuple bogeys at the 18th in successive rounds set up Boavista's closing hole as the week's house of horrors.

competitions.name: The Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Baker started R1 eight adrift of a rampant Mullin (49 pts, b03), trimmed the gap to five with a personal-best 45 at Palmares Praia/Alvor (b05), then overhauled Mullin outright at R3 H6 (b22) en route to another PB of 47 (b06). Gregg Williams briefly snatched the lead back at R3 H13 (b26) and drew level again at R4 H5 (b43), but Baker's record-breaking 51 at Boavista (b02) — built on a 21-point burst across holes 5-10 (b04) — buried the field by 11. Final: 184 pts, the highest in TEG history (b01).

competitions.name: The Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Mullin led the Gross from R1 H6 onwards and was never headed: rounds of +4, +11, +23, +13 (despite a quad at R3's 18th, b31) for +51 total, a 12-shot canter over runner-up Jon Baker. Even on a week when his Stableford crown slipped, his ball-striking didn't.

competitions.name: The Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Bottom of the Stableford table from R1 H4 to the final putt. Quintuple at the 8th in R1 (b17), a 10 at the 1st in R2 (b08), another quint at R3's 8th (b18), a 19-shot bleed across R3 holes 11-15 (b28), an 11 at R4's 8th (b09), and a closing 21-point round (b25 — his worst to date) sealed a 112-point Spoon (b21).

headline_candidates: Mullin Goes Bang: 49 Points and an Eight-Shot Lead at Boavista
The Day David Built His Cushion
rounds.chosen_headline: Mullin Goes Bang: 49 Points and an Eight-Shot Lead at Boavista
rounds.angle: Mullin's opener (b03) — the 2nd-best round in TEG history at that point — torches Boavista and sets the chase. Patterson immediately settles into last (b17), Neumann's closing sextuple (b11) signals the week to come.

headline_candidates: Palmares Debuts and the PBs Pile Up
Praia/Alvor: Three Career-Bests in One Afternoon
The Chase Begins: Baker and Williams Trim the Gap
rounds.chosen_headline: Palmares Debuts and the PBs Pile Up
rounds.angle: On a new TEG course, three personal-best rounds in the same field: Gregg Williams' 48 (b07), Alex Baker's 45 (b05), while Patterson opens with a 10 (b08) and Neumann turns the back nine into demolition (b12, b23, b67).

headline_candidates: Baker Takes the Wheel at Palmares
Lead Changes Hands — Twice — and Then for Good
rounds.chosen_headline: Baker Takes the Wheel at Palmares
rounds.angle: Alex's third personal best in three rounds (b06, 47 pts) finally puts him in front; despite a 10 of his own at the 13th (b15) and a brief Williams takeover (b26), he closes R3 sharing the top. Patterson's grim afternoon (b18, b28) keeps the Spoon firmly his.

headline_candidates: Baker Burns Boavista: A New TEG Record
The Sunday That Rewrote the Book
rounds.angle: Alex Baker's 51 (b02) — fuelled by 21 points in six holes (b04) — is the highest round in TEG history, settles the Trophy by 11 (b01), Mullin closes out the Jacket (b10), Patterson posts his worst-ever round (b25) including an 11 at the 8th (b09) to confirm the Spoon (b21).

players.arc: Eight back, then five, then level, then gone: three consecutive personal bests culminating in the highest round in TEG history and the largest Trophy total ever recorded.
Set the bar with a 49 in R1, kept the Gross under lock and key all week, but watched the Trophy walk away from him on Sunday at Boavista.
A personal-best 48 at Palmares and two cameo turns at the top of the leaderboard, but late blow-ups at Boavista (b32, b33) ended the challenge — a fine runner-up nonetheless.
Briefly led both competitions on R1 H9, posted a tidy six-hole par run at Palmares (b14), and settled for Green Jacket runner-up.
Wire-to-wire Spoon: four blow-ups of quintuple or worse, a personal-worst 21 to close, 112 points in the book.
A one-man big-number generator — two sextuples at Boavista's 18th in R1, another at Palmares' 18th in R2, and a quintuple double-act at Praia/Alvor's 13th and 14th.

venue_notes: TEG's 4th visit to the Algarve (2017). Bookend the report at Boavista (the 11th and 12th TEG rounds at that venue) — the same parkland-with-Atlantic-views that Mullin tamed on Wednesday and Baker dismantled on Sunday. Middle two rounds are first-time TEG visits to Palmares (Robert Trent Jones Jr.): R2 the Praia/Alvor links-meets-parkland combo where the PBs flowed, R3 the more tree-lined Alvor/Lagos where Baker grabbed the Trophy. Use the new-course novelty to frame R2's PB outbreak.
